metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready — Gemini has no explicit terminal stop_reason; the parser INFERS turn_done from a final text-only 'gemini' message with no open tool calls (parser.go:209), which fires IsAgentDone primary path + classifier rule 2b → working→ready.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

This is the smallest tool-less round-trip, pinning the **end signal** specifically: a final assistant text message with no tool_use closes the turn and drives working→ready. It overlaps intentionally with `basic-turn`; the difference in intent is that this cell asserts the end-of-turn *mechanism*, regardless of which adapter-specific marker carries it.

### agent_supports: yes

Gemini CLI v0.46.0 runs a single conversational round-trip out of the box — one user prompt, one assistant reply, zero tool use. No special mode is needed; the canonical tool-less prompt ('Reply with exactly: hello world') is the smallest turn the agent can run. Live-verified on 2026-06-09 during adapter onboarding (real session reported model `gemini-3-flash-preview`, non-zero cost + tokens, returned to ready on turn completion); CLI installed at v0.46.0 (`gemini --version`).

### daemon_capability: full

Unlike Claude Code (an explicit terminal stop_reason allow-list) or Codex (a `task_complete` marker), **Gemini emits NO explicit end-of-turn marker**. The adapter INFERS the turn's end from message shape: `parseAssistant` (parser.go:209-211) reclassifies a 'gemini' assistant message whose `content` is non-empty AND whose `toolCalls` is empty to `EventType = "turn_done"`. That sets `LastEventType="turn_done"`, which makes `IsAgentDone()` return true via the **primary** path (session.go:553), and classifier **rule 2b** (state_classifier.go:70-72) transitions working→ready with reason 'agent finished turn → ready'. The opening user text prompt emits `user_message` + `ClearToolNames` (parser.go:155-157) → ready→working. There are no tool calls in this scenario, so the `HasOpenToolCall` guard in `IsAgentDone` never blocks the settle. Topology is exactly `ready → working → ready`, final state `ready`, zero tool calls — matching the scenario's `Tool calls: ≤ 0`.

The two negative cases that would WRONGLY settle are both held off and unit-tested: an empty streaming placeholder (`content==""`) stays `assistant_message` (parser_test.go:85-86, :99-104 for the tool-calling case), and a tool-calling message stays `assistant_message` — only the final text-only message becomes `turn_done` (parser_test.go:118-119). This is the precise positive case the turn-end-terminal-text scenario asserts, with its negatives pinned.

### driver_capability: ready

The interactive driver (`replaydata/agents/gemini-cli/driver-interactive.sh`) launches `gemini -y` under tmux and genuinely elicits `send slash wait_turn sleep` (its `DRIVE_ELICITS` constant). The recipe needs only `send` (the tool-less prompt) + `wait_turn` (block until the inferred turn_done) + a trailing `sleep` to let the daemon observe the working→ready settle before teardown. `wait_turn` mirrors the parser's classification exactly — it counts a 'gemini' message with non-empty content and no tool calls as the completed turn. Every step type the recipe touches is implemented; no stubbed primitive (interrupt/keys/reset_session/restart/resume/sigkill/exit_clean/start_session/session) is exercised, so the driver is `ready`. An INTERACTIVE recipe is used deliberately: a one-shot headless `gemini -p` run exits at turn completion, before the daemon can observe the terminal working→ready edge — the same shape that keeps the claudecode/codex/aider cells for this scenario interactive.
caveats: Overlaps heavily with `basic-turn` (same ready→working→ready arc, same recipe shape). The two cells differ in intent: basic-turn asserts the overall happy-path lifecycle + TotalTokens>0; turn-end-terminal-text specifically asserts that the terminal-text end signal closes the turn. The recipe is a deliberately tool-less prompt to keep the cell focused on the end signal.

Provisional on emission: daemon=full for the end signal is grounded in the parser read + unit tests + the 2026-06-09 live-verify, but the no-explicit-turn_done inference and the default boot model string are emission-dependent. record promotes the observations.model assertion from provisional to settled; if the live default differs, update the model string (not a daemon bug).

Gemini has NO explicit end-of-turn marker; turn_done is INFERRED from a final text-only 'gemini' message with no open tool calls (parser.go:209). Correct and unit-tested for the tool-free turn, but the settle is heuristic (message shape) rather than an in-band terminal-reason marker like Claude's end_turn. A future Gemini transcript shape that interleaves a final empty placeholder after the text could delay the settle until the next non-empty line.

cwd lives only in the transcript body (<session_context>), not the path/process; PID binding relies on the parser's cwd harvest + EnrichNewSession LastCWD fallback. Session identity, not the lifecycle/end-signal arc.

sources.note: parseAssistant (L209-211): a 'gemini' message with non-empty content and len(toolCalls)==0 → EventType="turn_done" (Gemini has no explicit end-of-turn marker; the settle is inferred from message shape). parseUser (L155-157): text prompt → user_message + ClearToolNames (ready→working).

IsAgentDone (L535-567): primary path returns true on LastEventType=="turn_done" (L553); HasOpenToolCall guard (L542) never blocks here since there are zero tool calls. Codex is excluded from the assistant/assistant_output fallback, so Gemini's turn_done is the authoritative settle.
Rule 2b (L70-72): when IsAgentDone() is true, working/waiting → ready with reason 'agent finished turn → ready'.

Positive case pinned (L118-119): final text-only message → turn_done. Negatives pinned: empty streaming placeholder stays assistant_message (L85-86), tool-calling message stays assistant_message (L99-104). turn_done still carries AssistantText (L122).

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"; gemini -y under tmux; wait_turn mirrors the parser's text-only/no-tool turn_done inference — every step the turn-end recipe needs (send+wait_turn+sleep) is implemented.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH (>=0.45.2) and authenticated via Google OAuth (oauth-personal, no API key); June-18 free-tier deadline
irrlichd is recording — run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
first-run trust-folder prompt is auto-accepted by the driver
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (Gemini mints its own sessionId; the daemon binds the launcher PID by cwd harvested from the bootstrap <session_context>)
verify: events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered for the launcher bound by cwd within ~2000 ms
events.jsonl contains exactly one state_transition to working and exactly one state_transition to ready for the session
events.jsonl contains NO tool_use events for the session
the final 'gemini' transcript message carries non-empty content and no toolCalls (the inferred turn_done)
final session state is ready; state never entered waiting
